name: Succinylcholine
executiveSummary: Ultra-rapid-acting depolarizing relaxant for intubation; contraindications in hyperkalemia and burn patients.
indications:
## Indications

- Rapid sequence intubation (RSI) when there is no contraindication.
- Electroconvulsive therapy (regimens).

## Precautions

- Hyperkalemia, rhabdomyolysis, extensive burns, periodic paralysis, muscular dystrophies.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: 1 mg/kg/dose. Then 0.3 to 0.6 mg/kg until the desired effect is achieved.
pediatrico.notes: Neuromuscular blocker, requires ventilatory support during administration, does not alter consciousness. May cause hypertension and hypotension, cardiac arrest, malignant hyperthermia, rash, sialorrhea, respiratory depression.

neonatal.dose: 3 mg/kg IV in RSI according to protocol.

stability:
## General

- Reconstitute and use immediately.

## Pediatric guide

- Store in the refrigerator. Stability according to the manufacturer.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

- Severe hyperkalemia, fasciculations, myalgia, bradycardia in children.
